pages.text: Superplasticizer: naphthalene-based superplasticizer with 25% water reduce ratio, produced by Wulong Company of Zhejiang. Nano-TiO 2: P25 produced by Degussa Company. Figs. 1 and 2 showed the X-ray diffraction curve and the scanning electron microscopy of nano-TiO 2 respectively. 2.2. Experiment method 2.2.1. Fluidity test The cement mortar was molded and conserved on the 20 /C176C condition according to the mix proportion of Table 1 . The water/binder ratio used for all mixtures was 0.5 and the sand/binder ratio was 3. The amount of slag powder and superplasiti- cizer were 10% and 0.5% by weight of binder respectively while nano-TiO 2 was in amount of 5% and 10%. The ﬂuidity tests of cement mortar were conducted accord- ing to GB/T 2419-2005 [14]. Fig. 3 showed the results of ﬂuidity test of different ce- ment mortar. 2.2.2. Mechanical properties test The bending and compressive strength tests of cement mortar were conducted according to GB/T 17671-1999 [15]. Figs. 4 and 5 showed the bending and compres- sive strength of different cement mortar respectively. 2.2.3. Orientation index of CH crystal Hussin and Poole [16] used XRD spectrum to analyze the orientation index of CH crystal. The diffraction angle 2 h of (1 0 1) crystal plane of CH crystal was 34.0 /C176 and that of (0 0 1) crystal plane was 18.1 /C176. If the (1 0 1) crystal plane was used as reference plane, the orientation index R of (0 0 1) crystal plane can be calculated 20 30 40 50 60 70 80 -500 0 500 1000 1500 2000 2500 3000 3500 4000 2-Theta ( o ) Intensity (cps) anatase rutile Fig. 1. Figs. 6 and 7 showed the orientation index of CH crystal at different ages and the connection curve between relative compres- sive strength and relative orientation index, respectively. 2.2.4. X-ray diffraction analysis Concrete was broken and mortar particles were picked and grinded to the res- idue ratio on the 45 lm sieve less than 2% at speciﬁc age. The XRD test was done by Kigahu D/Max-3B X-ray Diffraction Instrument. Table 2 showed the diffraction intensity of Ca(OH) 2 of different specimens. 2.2.5. Scanning electron microscope test The cement mortar for SEM was molded and conserved on the 20 /C176C condition. Then the cement mortar was broken into small fragments and the hydration was stopped by pure alcohol at speciﬁc age. Those small fragments were sprayed with gold for 60 s in SBC-12 Small Particles Sputtering Apparatus to make specimens conductive. Microscopic structure of specimens under different magniﬁcations was observed with HITACHI S-570. Figs. 8–11 showed the SEM photos of different cement mortar at 1 day. 3. Results and discussion Fig. 1 showed there are two kinds of crystal which named Ana- tase and Rutile in the nano-TiO 2 materials and the main crystal was Anatase. Research results indicated the photo-catalytic efﬁciency of mixed crystal was better than simplex crystal [17,18]. Fig. 2 indi- cated the size of nano-TiO 2 which ranged from 40 nm to 60 nm. Because of the high speciﬁc surface of nano-TiO 2, further more water was needed to enwrap the solid particle. So the ﬂu- idity of cement mortar decreased with the increasing content of nano-TiO2. Fig. 3 showed that the ﬂuidity of cement mortar de- creased by 21% or 40% when 5% or 10% cement was substituted by nano-TiO 2. Fig. 4 indicated that the early strength increased and the evening strength decreased with the increasing content of nano-TiO 2. For example, the compressive strength of cement mortar at 1 day increased by 46% or 47% and the compressive strength at 28 day decreased by 6% or 9% when 5% or 10% ce- ment was substituted by nano-TiO 2. The strength of specimen P5 at 1 day was almost the same as that of specimen P10, but the ﬂuidity and strength of specimen P5 at 28 day were much better. That meant too much substitution content of nano-TiO 2 was not necessary. In order to enhance the ﬂuidity and the evening strength, superplasticizer and slag powder were added into cement mortar together with nano-TiO 2. Figs. 4 and 5 showed the bending and compressive strength of different specimens respectively. The compressive strength of specimen P5K10 and P10K10 at 1 day in- creased by 60% and 68% respectively and the compressive strength at 28 day also increased by 15% and 7% comparing with that of sample J. So superplasticizer and slag powder was helpful to mod- ify the evening strength of cement mortar with nano-TiO 2 even if the improvement on ﬂuidity was not obvious. Table 2 showed the diffraction intensity of CH and C 3S crystal of different specimens. Firstly, the intensity of diffraction apex of CH of specimen J increased and that of C 3S decreased along with the increasing hydration age. But C 3S still had not hydrated entirely even at 28 day. Secondly, the intensity of two diffraction peaks of CH showed entirely different change tendency such as the different XRD values of specimen J and P5. When the cement was substi- tuted by nano-TiO 2, the intensity of (1 0 1) crystal plane of CH in- creased at the early ages but the intensity of (0 0 1) crystal plane decreased a lot. It seemed that the amount of CH crystal did not in- crease at 1 day when cement was substituted by nano-TiO 2. So the reason for the improvement of early strength should not be the increasing amount of hydrated products. Thirdly, when the slag powder was added into the cement mortar, the intensity of CH at evening ages increased so slowly that it decreased after 14 days and the intensity of CH was much lower than that of other speci- mens without slag powder. That meant CH was consumed by the hydration of slag powder and it was helpful to improve the strength at evening ages. Fig. 6 showed the orientation index of different specimens at different ages. Results indicated that the orientation index of spec- imen J at 1 day was very high and was close to 4. That is very harm- ful for the strength of cement mortar. Results also showed the orientation index was still high even at 28 days although the orien- tation index of specimen J decreased along with the increasing ages. When the cement was substituted by nano

Figs. 8 and 9 showed the SEM photos of specimen J and P5 respectively. Results showed that many actinomorphic crystal balls created at 1 day for the nucleation function of nano-TiO 2. So the orientation index of CH decreased obviously and the compressive strength of cement mortar with nano-TiO 2 increased a lot. Figs. 10 and 11 showed SEM photos of cement mortar with nano-TiO2, slag powder and superplasticizer. It indicated that the microstructure was more tightly and the actinomorphic crystal ball was ﬁner because of the dispersion effect of superplasticizer and the hydration of slag powder. So it was helpful to modify the ﬂuid- ity and strength at evening ages. 4. Conclusions Effect of nano-TiO 2 on mechanical properties and microstruc- ture of cement mortar was studied in this paper. 1. Test results indicated that when cement was partially substi- tuted by nano-TiO 2, the strength of cement mortar at early ages increased a lot and the ﬂuidity and strength at evening ages decreased obviously. The compressive strength of cement mor- tar with 5% or 10% nano-TiO 2 at 1 day both increased by about 45%. But the compressive strength of cement mortar at 28 days decreased by 10% and 19% respectively and the ﬂuidity decreased by 20% and 40% respectively when 5% and 10% cement was substituted by nano-TiO 2. 2. The main reason for the improvement of strength is the decrease and modiﬁcation of orientation index for the nucle- ation function, not the increasing amount of hydration prod- ucts. At the same time, the orientation index have an obviously effect on the strength of cement mortar. 3. Results also indicated that it was useful to modify the ﬂuidity and the strength at evening ages of the cement mortar with nano-TiO2 by adding superplasticizer and slag powder into cement mortar because of the dispersion effect of superplasti- cizer and the hydration of slag powder. The effect of TiO 2 nanoparticles on water permeability and thermal and mechanical properties of high strength self-compacting con

Effect of nano-TiO 2 on the mechanical properties of cement mortar Tao Meng a,⇑, Yachao Yu b, Xiaoqian Qian a, Shulin Zhan a, Kuangliang Qian a a College of Civil Engineering and Architecture, Zhejiang University, Hangzhou 310058, PR China b Construction Industry Institute of Baoye Group, Shaoxing 312030, PR China article info Article history: Received 5 May 2011 Received in revised form 2 September 2011 Accepted 2 October 2011 Available online 24 November 2011 Keywords: Nano-TiO 2 Mechanical properties Microstructure Nucleus function Orientation index abstract Effect of nano-TiO 2 on the mechanical properties of cement mortar was studied in this paper. Test results indicated that when cement was substituted by nano-TiO 2, the strength of cement mortar at early ages increased a lot and the ﬂuidity and strength at evening ages decreased obviously. The change tendency of diffraction intensity of (0 0 1) crystal plane was entirely different with that of (1 0 1) crystal plane. It seemed that the main reason for the improvement of strength is the decrease and modiﬁcation of orien- tation index for the nucleus function, not the increasing amount of hydration products. Results also indi- cated that it was useful to modify the ﬂuidity and the strength at evening ages of the cement mortar with nano-TiO 2 by adding superplasticizer and slag powder into cement mortar. /C2112011 Elsevier Ltd. All rights reserved. 1. Introduction Along with the advancement of nano-technology, nano-TiO 2 was widely studied in many ﬁelds including disposing the waste water and exhaust gas [1,2] and improving the mechanical proper- ties and durability of concrete [3,4]. Wu et al. [5] studied the photocatalytic process of nano-TiO 2 to alkaline dye under UV light. Test results showed that a little amount of nano-TiO 2 could decrease the concentration of dye by 40% after 2 h. The effects of concentration of nano-TiO 2 and dye rotation speed, PH value and temperature on photo-catalytic process were also studied. Hydroxy radical ( /C5OH) was created by the photocatalytic reaction of nano-TiO 2. This would bring on the change from NO x gas to NO /C0 3 ions [1]. More surface analysis meth- od such as XPS and AFM were efﬁcient to analyze the photocata- lytic process of nano-TiO 2. Kry´ sa et al. [6] showed that the nano-TiO2 ﬁlm which was created on the top of glass has a good photocatalytic effect on four familiar organic pollutants. Effect on anti-penetration and microstructure of concrete with nano-SiO2 was studied [7]. Test results showed that the density of concrete was improved by nano-SiO 2 under the same ﬂuidity of concrete because of the enhancement function of nano-SiO 2. The anti-penetration grade of concrete was also improved a lot. Nazari and Riahi [8–10] studied the effect of many nano-particles on the mechanics and microstructure of concrete. The main rea- sons for increasing the strength and durability of concrete are to accelerate C–S–H gel formation because of the increasing crystal- line Ca(OH) 2 amount especially at the early age of hydration and to decrease harmful pores as the nano-ﬁllers. The results of Li and Zhang indicated nano-SiO 2 and nano-TiO 2 reﬁned the pore structure of concrete and enhanced the resistance to chloride pen- etration of concrete [11,12]. Meguid also studied the reason of improvement of the tensile and shear strength of concrete for add- ing nano-particle by TEM and mechanical test and got the similar conclusion [13]. If nano-TiO
